channel.title: Frontier Tech Research
channel.description: Field guides to the technologies that will define the next decade. Each domain gets two episodes: first the machine — what physically exists, who builds it, where the money pools — then the argument: a committed view of how it unfolds, with dates attached.
channel.itunes:author: Frontier Tech Research

item.title: Sand, Light, and Electricity — The Machine Behind AI
item.description: A programmer asks an AI to fix a bug. In the half-second before the answer appears, her question crosses the most expensive machine human beings have ever built: an eight-million-dollar rack in Virginia, chips printed by light in Taiwan, a bus-sized machine from one small Dutch town, and electricity ordered three years in advance. A tour of the physical world behind artificial intelligence — how it works, who builds it, and where the money pools. Episode one of two.

item.title: Proofs, Tests, and Paychecks — When the Machine Becomes a Worker
item.description: The same AI is superhuman at mathematics a computer can check — and confidently wrong two-thirds of the time where no checker exists. That one experiment explains the decade ahead: the world is about to have far more machine intelligence than it can verify. Where cheap checkers exist, machines become workers on a schedule; where they don't, the work stays human. The argument, with dates attached — and exactly what would prove it wrong. Episode two of two.
